title: Conduit Traffic Allowlist Enforcement — Unexpected DNP3 Functions

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 5.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.47: Conduit Traffic Allowlist Enforcement — Unexpected DNP3 Functions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.47' running on edge_hub_dnp3 or dnp3:frame, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Edge Hub or serial concentrator logs
spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_dnp3" conduit_id="C-DMZ-OT1" earliest=-24h
| lookup dnp3_conduit_matrix.csv conduit_id function_code OUTPUT allowed
| where allowed!="true"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, conduit_id
| sort - count
description: Detects DNP3 function codes on encrypted OT tunnels that are not in the approved conduit function matrix.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_dnp3. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Document matrix per conduit; (2) test in lab; (3) alert and optional block at gateway; (4) annual matrix review with engineering. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_dnp3`; Lookups: `dnp3_conduit_matrix`, `dnp3_conduit_matrix.csv`.
visualization: Heatmap (function×conduit), Table, Time chart. Key fields: `conduit_id`, `dest_ip`, `function_code`, `src_ip`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for detects dnp3 function codes on encrypted ot tunnels that are not in the approved conduit function matrix. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Edge Hub or serial concentrator logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_dnp3` or `dnp3:frame`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Document matrix per conduit; (2) test in lab; (3) alert and optional block at gateway; (4) annual matrix review with engineering.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_dnp3" conduit_id="C-DMZ-OT1" earliest=-24h
| lookup dnp3_conduit_matrix.csv conduit_id function_code OUTPUT allowed
| where allowed!="true"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, conduit_id
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Conduit Traffic Allowlist Enforcement — Unexpected DNP3 Functions (IEC 62443-3-2 / Conduit traffic allowlist enforcement)** — Detects DNP3 function codes on encrypted OT tunnels that are not in the approved conduit function matrix.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_dnp3` or `dnp3:frame`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Edge Hub or serial concentrator logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_dnp3. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_dnp3", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where allowed!="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, conduit_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (function×conduit), Table, Time chart.
